commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of notification privacy/default behavior, not a narrow automated repair.; Needs attention: No supply-chain or credential-handling issue was found, but the intentional OS/browser notification metadata exposure needs maintainer acceptance. (sha=cf9378ed7b7f9305ae07b6e6f9638d1328b634ea)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Browser Notification API handling for incoming realtime message.created events, updates the embedded web bundle, and adds Playwright coverage for notification creation and click navigation.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for this PR as a feature implementation. The linked issue is source-reproducible on current main because realtime message handling exists but no browser notification path is present.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of notification privacy/default behavior, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat(web): add browser notifications for incoming messages
target.body:
## Summary

Closes openclaw/clickclack#25.

This adds a narrow browser-notification path in the web app for incoming realtime `message.created` events:

- only runs when the browser `Notification` API exists and permission is already `granted`
- skips the current user's own messages
- skips the active conversation while the document is visible
- shows a small notification for messages arriving elsewhere
- focuses the app and navigates to the relevant channel or DM when clicked

Pushover remains unchanged. This intentionally does not add service-worker/offline push, a permission prompt UI, or `agent.progress`/busy-indicator behavior.

AI-assisted PR: yes. I understand the changed code path; this is wired into the existing realtime event handler and uses the existing channel/DM navigation helpers.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/chat.spec.ts -g "browser notifications announce"`
- `pnpm check`

Local Codex review was attempted with `codex review --base origin/main`, but the default model was unavailable to this project; retrying with `-c model="gpt-5.4"` hit quota. I did a manual final diff pass after the test/build gates.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  - Incoming realtime messages outside the active visible conversation now create a browser notification when browser notification permission is already granted.
- Real environment tested:
  - Local ClickClack app served by the repository Playwright web server on `http://127.0.0.1:18082`, with the real Go API, real realtime path, real web bundle, and dev-bootstrap data.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/chat.spec.ts -g "browser notifications announce"`
  - `pnpm check`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - Focused e2e passed: `1 passed (6.8s)`.
  - The e2e creates a real channel, starts the app, sends a real remote message through `POST /api/channels/{id}/messages`, captures the web app's `Notification` constructor call, and invokes the notification click handler.
  - Full local gate passed: web build, SDK build, Go tests, root/workspace typechecks, oxlint, gofmt check, and oxfmt check.
- Observed result after fix:
  - The app created one notification titled for the target channel with the ClickClack icon.
  - Invoking the notification click handler navigated the app from `#general` to the channel that received the message.
- What was not tested:
  - Native OS notification rendering outside Playwright.
  - Requesting notification permission from the app UI.
  - Service-worker/offline Push API behavior after the browser is closed.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  - The automated proof fakes the browser `Notification` constructor so it can assert the app call deterministically in headless Chromium.
  - Current realtime `message.created` payloads do not include message body text, so notifications use a generic `New message` body unless future event payloads include a body.



## Native Windows / Edge notification proof

A real Windows recording was checked after the mock-only Playwright proof. The important toast appears at about **17 seconds** into the clip; this is the ClickClack/Edge toast, not the later Discord toast.

- Proof notes and matching ClickClack DB/event/log data: <https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PollyBot13/clickclack/proof/clickclack-pr-26-browser-notification/proof/pr-26-browser-notification/proof.md>
- Still frame at ~17s showing native Windows toast from **ClickClack**, **via Microsoft Edge**, title `PollyBot Ops in #general`, body `New message`: <https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PollyBot13/clickclack/proof/clickclack-pr-26-browser-notification/proof/pr-26-browser-notification/01-edge-clickclack-toast-at-17s.jpg>
- Still frame after clicking the toast showing Edge/ClickClack focused on the target channel `#general`: <https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PollyBot13/clickclack/proof/clickclack-pr-26-browser-notification/proof/pr-26-browser-notification/02-click-result-channel-at-20s.jpg>

Backing data for the same proof message:

```text
message_id: msg_01kv6ewydvh1a1j2m3kqmg998g
event_id: evt_01kv6ewydwc6ftmyn9ftxbpzpq
event_type: message.created
event_seq: 21
channel_id: chn_01kv60tay7c20jzw1n5qsfmj9p
channel: general
created_at: 2026-06-15T20:17:21Z
server_log: POST /api/channels/chn_01kv60tay7c20jzw1n5qsfmj9p/messages -> 201 at local 22:17:21
```

Observed result: Windows showed a native ClickClack notification delivered via Microsoft Edge, and clicking it focused Edge/ClickClack on `#general`, the channel for the proof message. This still does not claim service-worker/offline push; it is the intended open-browser/PWA notification path after notification permission has already been granted.
target.author: pollybot13

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
